description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, blocky stone with a predominantly light gray to off-white coloration, speckled with numerous small, dark mineral inclusions. The stone's surface is uneven and jagged, with some faces appearing more angular and others more irregularly fractured. One side features a black-and-white scale bar marked '3 cm' and handwritten text, suggesting it has been cataloged or studied, possibly for geological or urban planning purposes. The stone's texture is visibly coarse, with a matte, granular finish that scatters light rather than reflecting it. There are subtle yellowish stains on one face, indicating minor weathering or mineral staining. The overall appearance suggests a durable, igneous or metamorphic rock commonly used as an obstacle or barrier in urban environments.
description: A blocky, light gray stone with dark speckles and a coarse, matte surface, featuring angular and irregular faces. It has a visible scale bar and minor yellowish staining.
description_view_0: A top-down view showing the broad, flat face of the stone with scattered dark mineral specks and a slight yellowish stain near one edge.
description_view_1: An angled view highlighting the stone's thickness, the black-and-white scale bar marked '3 cm', and handwritten text along one edge.
description_view_2: A side view focusing on the stone's rough, irregular surface and the prominent scale bar, with more visible dark inclusions.
description_view_3: A view of the stone's most angular, jagged face, showing the coarse texture and absence of the scale bar.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a small barrier along paths
attribute_pattern: Randomly distributed dark mineral speckles on a light gray background, with no regular or artificial patterning.
attribute_weathering: Slightly weathered with minor yellowish staining, but overall appears freshly broken with sharp edges and a coarse surface.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements in urban green spaces.
